title: HEATED
description: A newsletter for people who are pissed off about the climate crisis.

items.title: What should we ask the plastic doctor?
items.description: I'm interviewing her tomorrow!

items.title: Chevron's CEO made $104 million while America bombed Iran
items.description: America's oil executives have pocketed $1.4 billion selling stock during the Iran war, a new investigation shows.

items.title: Oil worker says fracking waste eroded his jaw
items.description: An elementary school was built atop the site where the radioactive waste was spread, journalist Saul Elbein reports.

items.title: Why smart people believe myths about electric cars
items.description: EVs should be having their moment, but misinformation is standing in the way. We spoke to a researcher about how to course correct.

items.title: What oil actually costs
items.description: The market price of oil has never reflected its true cost. This week made that impossible to ignore.

items.title: "Is it a condom or is it gasoline?"
items.description: Tracy and I play a game.

items.title: How fossil fuel ads manipulate us
items.description: On today’s podcast, we watch and analyze fossil fuel ads.

items.title: Fossil fuel propaganda is evolving
items.description: A new analysis of nearly 2,000 fossil fuel ads finds Big Oil has moved from green promises to insisting oil and gas are inevitable. (They're not).

items.title: Our reaction to the Steyer interview
items.description: Just some immediate, off-the-cuff thoughts.

items.title: Can a billionaire fix California?
items.description: Tom Steyer argues he's not a regular billionaire. He's a cool billionaire.

items.title: NBC's top climate reporter resigns
items.description: In an exclusive interview, veteran NBC meteorologist Chase Cain opens up about burnout, suppression, and why he's going independent.

items.title: Climate coverage is shrinking. We're expanding it.
items.description: Announcing a new weekly video podcast!

items.title: Sam Alito has an oil money problem
items.description: Nearly 30 percent of Alito’s individual stock portfolio is directly tied to fossil fuels.

items.title: Trump is wiping out all climate regulation. Big Oil may regret it.
items.description: For decades, oil majors fought climate regulation. Now, they’re afraid Trump’s extreme rollbacks could leave them on the hook to pay for what they’ve done.

items.title: Actually, I do know how to do this
items.description: In retrospect, paying attention to polluters may be one of the best ways to understand what’s currently happening in the United States.

items.title: I don't know how to do this
items.description: But I'm going to keep doing it anyway.

items.title: Air pollution denial is now EPA policy
items.description: Dirty air may kill people, but Trump's EPA won't count the bodies.

items.title: RFK Jr. forgot what makes us healthy
items.description: Healthy food is not healthy if it destroys the environment that produces it.

items.title: It's time to embrace climate conspiracy
items.description: Trump’s Venezuela oil play exposes what climate reporting has documented for decades—if we’re willing to say it out loud.

items.title: Exxon’s new greenwashing ploy
items.description: The oil giant claims it can eliminate more than 90 percent of emissions from gas-powered AI facilities. Critics say that’s nonsense.
